title: I'm a neuroscience researcher. Here's how AI changed my workflow
post:
I was skeptical about AI in research. Then I integrated it into my daily workflow and haven't looked back.

It handles the tedium: transcribing interviews, summarizing patient notes, formatting citations, drafting methodology sections. Meanwhile, I focus on what actually requires human judgment: designing experiments, interpreting ambiguous results, and thinking through implications. My papers are getting stronger because I'm spending time on the parts that matter.

The researchers who resist AI aren't protecting rigor — they're sacrificing efficiency for ideology.

How has AI changed your research? Thoughts?
